#'   rarefaction.
#'
#' @inheritParams clean_pq
#' @param sample_size (integer) the depth to rarefy to. If `NULL` (default),
#'   the minimum `sample_sums(physeq)` is used. Samples with fewer reads than
#'   `sample_size` are dropped.
#' @param n (integer, default 1) number of rarefaction repetitions to average
#'   over. Values > 1 return a non-integer (averaged) OTU table.
#' @param seed (integer, default 123) random seed. Set to `FALSE` to leave the
#'   random number generator untouched (i.e. use the current RNG state),
#'   mirroring the `rngseed` argument of [phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()].
#' @param replace (logical, default FALSE) sample with replacement? `FALSE`
#'   (without replacement) is the recommended, less biased default. `TRUE`
#'   reproduces the default behaviour of [phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()].
#' @param ... Not used. Kept for backward compatibility.
#'
#' @details
#'   Rarefaction is performed internally rather than by calling
#'   [phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()], whose `replace = FALSE` code path relies
#'   on `rep_len(x["OTUi"], x["times"])` and errors with `invalid 'length.out'
#'   value` under recent R-devel (see phyloseq issue \#1753). For a single
#'   rarefaction (`n = 1`), the result is identical to
#'   [phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()] with `trimOTUs = FALSE` for the same
#'   `seed`, `sample_size` and `replace` — except in the degenerate case where
#'   a retained sample has a single read, in which phyloseq triggers a
#'   `sample()` edge-case bug that `rarefy_pq()` avoids. Empty OTUs are never
#'   trimmed.

#' sample_sums(data_f_rar5)

content.content:
1	# Verification: is MiscMetabar's internal rarefaction (`rarefy_even_depth_pq`,
2	# used by `rarefy_pq()`) equivalent to `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()`?
3	#
4	# Claim being tested: for a single rarefaction the two are BIT-IDENTICAL for the
5	# same `seed`, `sample_size` and `replace` (with `trimOTUs = FALSE`), EXCEPT in
6	# the degenerate case where a retained sample has a single read — there phyloseq
7	# hits a `sample()` edge-case bug (`sample(x, 1)` with scalar `x > 1` returns a
8	# random index, not `x`) that `rarefy_even_depth_pq()` avoids.
9	#
10	# Run with R 4.6.0 (release): Rscript dev/verify_rarefy_equivalence.R
11	
12	.libPaths(path.expand("~/R/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu-library/4.6"))
13	suppressMessages(devtools::load_all(".", quiet = TRUE))
14	suppressMessages(library(phyloseq))
15	
16	cat(
17	  "R:",
18	  R.version.string,
19	  "| phyloseq:",
20	  as.character(packageVersion("phyloseq")),
21	  "\n\n"
22	)
23	
24	# phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth() saves/restores `.Random.seed`; ensure it exists.
25	if (!exists(".Random.seed", envir = globalenv(), inherits = FALSE)) {
26	  set.seed(1)
27	}
28	
29	# Coerce an otu_table to a taxa-as-rows numeric matrix, ordered by name, so two
30	# phyloseq objects can be compared regardless of internal orientation/order.
31	as_canon <- function(ps) {
32	  m <- as(otu_table(ps), "matrix")
33	  if (!taxa_are_rows(ps)) {
34	    m <- t(m)
35	  }
36	  m <- m[order(rownames(m)), order(colnames(m)), drop = FALSE]
37	  storage.mode(m) <- "double"
38	  m
39	}
40	
41	# Compare a phyloseq run against a MiscMetabar run; returns a one-row summary.
42	compare <- function(ps, sample_size, replace, seed, label, trimOTUs = FALSE) {
43	  ss <- if (is.null(sample_size)) min(sample_sums(ps)) else sample_size
44	
45	  if (isFALSE(seed)) {
46	    # Unseeded: set the SAME RNG state before each to test identical consumption.
47	    set.seed(20260604)
48	    a <- suppressMessages(phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth(
49	      ps,
50	      sample.size = ss,
51	      rngseed = FALSE,
52	      replace = replace,
53	      trimOTUs = trimOTUs,
54	      verbose = FALSE
55	    ))
56	    set.seed(20260604)
57	    b <- rarefy_even_depth_pq(
58	      ps,
59	      ss,
60	      rngseed = FALSE,
61	      replace = replace,
62	      trimOTUs = trimOTUs
63	    )
64	  } else {
65	    a <- suppressMessages(phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth(
66	      ps,
67	      sample.size = ss,
68	      rngseed = seed,
69	      replace = replace,
70	      trimOTUs = trimOTUs,
71	      verbose = FALSE
72	    ))
73	    b <- rarefy_even_depth_pq(
74	      ps,
75	      ss,
76	      rngseed = seed,
77	      replace = replace,
78	      trimOTUs = trimOTUs
79	    )
80	  }
81	
82	  ma <- as_canon(a)
83	  mb <- as_canon(b)
84	  same_samples <- setequal(sample_names(a), sample_names(b))
85	  same_taxa <- setequal(taxa_names(a), taxa_names(b))
86	  same_dims <- identical(dim(ma), dim(mb))
87	  identical_counts <- same_dims && all(ma == mb)
88	  data.frame(
89	    case = label,
90	    replace = replace,
91	    sample_size = ss,
92	    seed = as.character(seed),
93	    trim = trimOTUs,
94	    ntaxa_phylo = ntaxa(a),
95	    ntaxa_pq = ntaxa(b),
96	    same_samples = same_samples,
97	    same_taxa = same_taxa,
98	    same_dims = same_dims,
99	    identical = identical_counts,
100	    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
101	  )
102	}
103	
104	dfm <- data_fungi_mini
105	dfm_col <- taxa_as_columns(dfm)
106	dfm_sub <- prune_samples(sample_names(dfm)[1:30], dfm) # heterogeneous depths
107	
108	grid <- expand.grid(
109	  replace = c(TRUE, FALSE),
110	  sample_size = c(500, 1000),
111	  seed = c(1, 123),
112	  trimOTUs = c(TRUE, FALSE),
113	  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
114	)
115	
116	cat("=== Non-degenerate cases (expect identical = TRUE everywhere) ===\n")
117	res <- list()
118	for (i in seq_len(nrow(grid))) {
119	  g <- grid[i, ]
120	  res[[length(res) + 1]] <- compare(
121	    dfm,
122	    g$sample_size,
123	    g$replace,
124	    g$seed,
125	    "taxa_rows",
126	    trimOTUs = g$trimOTUs
127	  )
128	  res[[length(res) + 1]] <- compare(
129	    dfm_col,
130	    g$sample_size,
131	    g$replace,
132	    g$seed,
133	    "taxa_cols",
134	    trimOTUs = g$trimOTUs
135	  )
136	}
137	# unseeded + a smaller subset for extra coverage (incl. heavy trimming at 1000)
138	res[[length(res) + 1]] <- compare(dfm, 500, TRUE, FALSE, "unseeded_repl")
139	res[[length(res) + 1]] <- compare(dfm, 500, FALSE, FALSE, "unseeded_norepl")
140	res[[length(res) + 1]] <- compare(dfm_sub, NULL, TRUE, 1, "subset_minss_repl")
141	res[[length(res) + 1]] <- compare(
142	  dfm_sub,
143	  800,
144	  FALSE,
145	  7,
146	  "subset_norepl",
147	  trimOTUs = TRUE
148	)
149	
150	tab <- do.call(rbind, res)
151	print(tab, row.names = FALSE)
152	n_id <- sum(tab$identical)
153	cat(sprintf("\nIdentical: %d / %d non-degenerate cases\n", n_id, nrow(tab)))
154

# Verification: is MiscMetabar's internal rarefaction (`rarefy_even_depth_pq`,
# used by `rarefy_pq()`) equivalent to `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()`?
#
# Claim being tested: for a single rarefaction the two are BIT-IDENTICAL for the
# same `seed`, `sample_size` and `replace` (with `trimOTUs = FALSE`), EXCEPT in
# the degenerate case where a retained sample has a single read — there phyloseq
# hits a `sample()` edge-case bug (`sample(x, 1)` with scalar `x > 1` returns a
# random index, not `x`) that `rarefy_even_depth_pq()` avoids.
#
# Run with R 4.6.0 (release): Rscript dev/verify_rarefy_equivalence.R

.libPaths(path.expand("~/R/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu-library/4.6"))
suppressMessages(devtools::load_all(".", quiet = TRUE))
suppressMessages(library(phyloseq))

cat(
  "R:",
  R.version.string,
  "| phyloseq:",
  as.character(packageVersion("phyloseq")),
  "\n\n"
)

# phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth() saves/restores `.Random.seed`; ensure it exists.
if (!exists(".Random.seed", envir = globalenv(), inherits = FALSE)) {
  set.seed(1)
}

# Coerce an otu_table to a taxa-as-rows numeric matrix, ordered by name, so two
# phyloseq objects can be compared regardless of internal orientation/order.
as_canon <- function(ps) {
  m <- as(otu_table(ps), "matrix")
  if (!taxa_are_rows(ps)) {
    m <- t(m)
  }
  m <- m[order(rownames(m)), order(colnames(m)), drop = FALSE]
  storage.mode(m) <- "double"
  m
}

# Compare a phyloseq run against a MiscMetabar run; returns a one-row summary.
compare <- function(ps, sample_size, replace, seed, label, trimOTUs = FALSE) {
  ss <- if (is.null(sample_size)) min(sample_sums(ps)) else sample_size

  if (isFALSE(seed)) {
    # Unseeded: set the SAME RNG state before each to test identical consumption.
    set.seed(20260604)
    a <- suppressMessages(phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth(
      ps,
      sample.size = ss,
      rngseed = FALSE,
      replace = replace,
      trimOTUs = trimOTUs,
      verbose = FALSE
    ))
    set.seed(20260604)
    b <- rarefy_even_depth_pq(
      ps,
      ss,
      rngseed = FALSE,
      replace = replace,
      trimOTUs = trimOTUs
    )
  } else {
    a <- suppressMessages(phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth(
      ps,
      sample.size = ss,
      rngseed = seed,
      replace = replace,
      trimOTUs = trimOTUs,
      verbose = FALSE
    ))
    b <- rarefy_even_depth_pq(
      ps,
      ss,
      rngseed = seed,
      replace = replace,
      trimOTUs = trimOTUs
    )
  }

  ma <- as_canon(a)
  mb <- as_canon(b)
  same_samples <- setequal(sample_names(a), sample_names(b))
  same_taxa <- setequal(taxa_names(a), taxa_names(b))
  same_dims <- identical(dim(ma), dim(mb))
  identical_counts <- same_dims && all(ma == mb)
  data.frame(
    case = label,
    replace = replace,
    sample_size = ss,
    seed = as.character(seed),
    trim = trimOTUs,
    ntaxa_phylo = ntaxa(a),
    ntaxa_pq = ntaxa(b),
    same_samples = same_samples,
    same_taxa = same_taxa,
    same_dims = same_dims,
    identical = identical_counts,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

dfm <- data_fungi_mini
dfm_col <- taxa_as_columns(dfm)
dfm_sub <- prune_samples(sample_names(dfm)[1:30], dfm) # heterogeneous depths

grid <- expand.grid(
  replace = c(TRUE, FALSE),
  sample_size = c(500, 1000),
  seed = c(1, 123),
  trimOTUs = c(TRUE, FALSE),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

cat("=== Non-degenerate cases (expect identical = TRUE everywhere) ===\n")
res <- list()
for (i in seq_len(nrow(grid))) {
  g <- grid[i, ]
  res[[length(res) + 1]] <- compare(
    dfm,
    g$sample_size,
    g$replace,
    g$seed,
    "taxa_rows",
    trimOTUs = g$trimOTUs
  )
  res[[length(res) + 1]] <- compare(
    dfm_col,
    g$sample_size,
    g$replace,
    g$seed,
    "taxa_cols",
    trimOTUs = g$trimOTUs
  )
}
# unseeded + a smaller subset for extra coverage (incl. heavy trimming at 1000)
res[[length(res) + 1]] <- compare(dfm, 500, TRUE, FALSE, "unseeded_repl")
res[[length(res) + 1]] <- compare(dfm, 500, FALSE, FALSE, "unseeded_norepl")
res[[length(res) + 1]] <- compare(dfm_sub, NULL, TRUE, 1, "subset_minss_repl")
res[[length(res) + 1]] <- compare(
  dfm_sub,
  800,
  FALSE,
  7,
  "subset_norepl",
  trimOTUs = TRUE
)

tab <- do.call(rbind, res)
print(tab, row.names = FALSE)
n_id <- sum(tab$identical)
cat(sprintf("\nIdentical: %d / %d non-degenerate cases\n", n_id, nrow(tab)))

cat("\n=== Degenerate case: sample_size = 1 (min sum), replace = FALSE ===\n")
cat(
  "A single-read sample must keep its one read in its ORIGINAL OTU. phyloseq's\n"
)
cat("`sample(x, 1)` scalar gotcha instead scatters it to a random OTU.\n")
deg <- compare(dfm, 1, FALSE, 1, "degenerate_ss1")
print(deg, row.names = FALSE)

# Focus on samples that hold exactly one read: which OTU ends up with that read?
otu_canon <- as_canon(dfm)
single <- colnames(otu_canon)[colSums(otu_canon) == 1]
true_otu <- apply(otu_canon[, single, drop = FALSE], 2, which.max)

b_deg <- rarefy_even_depth_pq(
  dfm,
  1,
  rngseed = 1,
  replace = FALSE,
  trimOTUs = FALSE
)
a_deg <- suppressMessages(phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth(
  dfm,
  sample.size = 1,
  rngseed = 1,
  replace = FALSE,
  trimOTUs = FALSE,
  verbose = FALSE
))
b_otu <- apply(as_canon(b_deg)[, single, drop = FALSE], 2, which.max)
a_otu <- apply(as_canon(a_deg)[, single, drop = FALSE], 2, which.max)

cat(sprintf("Single-read samples: %d\n", length(single)))
cat(sprintf(
  "MiscMetabar keeps read in original OTU: %d / %d\n",
  sum(b_otu == true_otu),
  length(single)
))
cat(sprintf(
  "phyloseq    keeps read in original OTU: %d / %d  (< %d => phyloseq bug)\n",
  sum(a_otu == true_otu),
  length(single),
  length(single)
))

cat("\n=== RNG-state restoration (phyloseq saves/restores .Random.seed) ===\n")
rng_ok <- TRUE
for (rep in c(TRUE, FALSE)) {
  set.seed(42)
  s0 <- .Random.seed
  invisible(suppressMessages(phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth(
    dfm,
    sample.size = 500,
    rngseed = 7,
    replace = rep,
    trimOTUs = TRUE,
    verbose = FALSE
  )))
  phylo_restores <- identical(s0, .Random.seed)
  set.seed(42)
  s0 <- .Random.seed
  invisible(rarefy_even_depth_pq(dfm, 500, rngseed = 7, replace = rep))
  pq_restores <- identical(s0, .Random.seed)
  match <- phylo_restores == pq_restores
  rng_ok <- rng_ok && match
  cat(sprintf(
    "replace=%-5s | phyloseq restores: %s | pq restores: %s | match: %s\n",
    rep,
    phylo_restores,
    pq_restores,
    match
  ))
}

cat("\n================ VERDICT ================\n")
ok <- n_id == nrow(tab) && rng_ok
cat(
  if (ok) {
    "PASS: MiscMetabar is bit-identical to phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth() in all\nnon-degenerate cases, and correct (unlike phyloseq) in the degenerate case.\n"
  } else {
    "FAIL: divergence found in a non-degenerate case (see `identical = FALSE`).\n"
  }
)

lastPrompt: Please explain me again the bug resolve by the rarefy_even_depth_pq function in degenerate case

aiTitle: Convert rarefy_even_depth_pq to public function with documentation

content.text:
Here is the explanation:

---

## The degenerate-case bug in `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()`

### What rarefaction without replacement does

When rarefying without replacement (`replace = FALSE`), the algorithm must draw `sample_size` reads uniformly at random from the reads present in a sample. The standard way to do this is:

1. Build an expanded vector where each OTU index appears as many times as its count: `c(OTU1, OTU1, OTU2, OTU2, OTU2, ...)`.
2. Draw `sample_size` indices from that expanded vector: `sample.int(length(expanded), sample_size)`.
3. Count how many times each OTU was drawn.

### What phyloseq does instead (and why it breaks)

phyloseq uses a shortcut. Instead of building the expanded vector with `rep()`, it calls:

```r
sample(x["OTUi"], x["times"])
```

where `x["OTUi"]` is the OTU index (an integer, say `3`) and `x["times"]` is the count (how many reads to draw, say `1`).

The intent is `sample(3, 1)` — *"draw 1 element from the vector `c(3)`"* — but `sample()` in R has a special rule: **when its first argument is a scalar integer `n > 1`, it interprets it as `1:n`** and samples from that range, not from the single-element vector.

So `sample(3, 1)` returns a random value from `{1, 2, 3}` rather than `3`. In the normal case (many reads per sample) this does not matter because many OTUs compete and the statistics average out. But in the **degenerate case where a sample has exactly 1 read**, there is exactly one OTU with a count of 1. phyloseq calls `sample(otu_index, 1)` and instead of keeping the read in that OTU, it scatters it to a random OTU between 1 and `otu_index`. The read ends up in the wrong taxon.

### What `rarefy_even_depth_pq` does instead

```r
expanded <- rep(seq_along(x), times = as.integer(x))
idx <- expanded[sample.int(length(expanded), sample_size)]
tabulate(idx, nbins = length(x))
```

It builds the full expanded index vector explicitly with `rep()`, then draws from it with `sample.int()`. When a sample has a single read in OTU `k`, `expanded` is just `c(k)`, `sample.int(1, 1)` always returns `1`, and `expanded[1]` is `k`. The read always stays in its original OTU. No scalar-`sample()` ambiguity.

### When does the bug matter in practice?

Only when `sample_size = 1` (or when a sample's total read count equals `sample_size` and only one OTU has any reads — effectively the same edge case). In typical metabarcoding analyses you rarefy to hundreds or thousands of reads, so the bug never triggers. It is nonetheless incorrect by construction and could surface if you rarefy to a very low depth or work with sparse synthetic data.
